title: erycibe subglabra
intro: Erycibe subglabra is a plant species belonging to the family Convolvulaceae, commonly known as the morning glory family. It is a type of climber found in Southeast Asia, used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antimalarial, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Erycibe subglabra include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections, making it a valuable herbal remedy in the region. In terms of herbal preparations, Erycibe subglabra can be consumed in the form of decoctions, infusions, or even extracts, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized effectively.
